title: lupulus lupuloides var. pubescens
intro: Lupulus lupuloides var. pubescens, also known as hop cone, is a variety of the hop plant that is native to Europe and is prized for its medicinal and herbal properties. This plant is used to create a range of herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their anti-inflammatory and sedative properties. The health benefits of Lupulus lupuloides var. pubescens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote relaxation and sleep. Traditionally, the plant has been used to create herbal remedies for a range of ailments, including insomnia, restlessness, and digestive problems.
benefits: Lupulus lupuloides var. pubescens has many benefits, such as reducing inflammation and pain, improving cardiovascular health by lowering cholesterol and blood pressure, and enhancing digestive health by reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. This plant has also been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, fighting off infections and promoting wound healing. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. By incorporating this plant into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall well-being, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a longer and healthier life.
intro_study: According to "Nutrition research and practice", lupulus lupuloides var. pubescens, also known as tricin, is a component of Lomens-P0 that inhibits prolactin secretion and is expected to be effective in improving premenstrual syndrome (PMS) symptoms.
constituents: Lupulus lupuloides var. pubescens has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, that possess anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant also contains phenolic acids like ferulic and sinapic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, improving heart health and protecting against infections. Additionally, lupuloides var. pubescens contains terpenoids, including beta-pinene and alpha-pinene, that have been found to have bronchodilatory effects, providing relief for individuals suffering from asthma and other respiratory disorders. The presence of these medicinal constituents in lupuloides var. pubescens contributes to its potential use in treating various health conditions, ultimately enhancing the quality of life for those who utilize it.
